findings.title: Add LRU caching to prompting algorithm LLM calls
findings.description: All 5 prompting algorithms (TRM Refinement, Adaptive Expansion, Metacognitive Monitor, Signal-Aware Thought Buffer, Collaborative Inference) make redundant LLM calls when processing similar prompts within the same pipeline run. For example, during a Stage 4 PRD generation with 3 retries, the same confidence evaluation calls are repeated with identical inputs. Each LLM call costs ~2-5 seconds via CLI and consumes tokens via API. A shared LRU cache keyed on (prompt_hash, context_hash, model) across all algorithms would eliminate redundant calls. Cache should be per-session, bounded (max 128 entries), invalidated on model change, and transparent to algorithms (decorator pattern).
evidence: 3 retry cycles * 5 confidence evaluations each = 15 redundant LLM calls per finding
Average CLI LLM call latency: 3.2 seconds (measured over 100 calls)
Estimated time savings per finding: 48 seconds (15 * 3.2s)
